Knowledge_Instances.name: Restricted Area
Knowledge_Instances.desc:
Contractors might find complexes belonging to various factions on the surface of planets.

These mostly buried facilities are accessible through a surface elevator, that may sometimes be locked by an access key.

Such area contain restricted data that you need to find to complete the area. Leaving the area without completing it first will reset the contractor's inventory to its state before entering.

A completed area can no longer be visited.

B_ControlBase.name: Base Command Center
B_ControlBase.desc: This central building provides the basic storage, electric power and administrative systems needed to operate a small land base. Deploying a base constitutes a legal claim over the area and deposits it is placed over. It also comes with basic manual smelting and crafting machines.

B_ControlBase2.name: Command Tower
B_ControlBase2.desc: This building allows a base to expand over a Level 2 land area, doubling its maximum footprint and unlocking experience thresholds up to 800 points.

B_ControlBase3PH.name: Command Relay
B_ControlBase3PH.desc: This building allows a base to expand over a Level 3 land area, doubling its maximum footprint and unlocking experience thresholds up to 2000 points.

B_ControlBase_Corpo1.name: Corporation Command Center
B_ControlBase_Corpo1.desc: A legally sanctionned corporation of importance often requires a place to centralized its operation. This building increases the maximum size of a single base of the corporation and contains crafting machines, a conference room, and many customizable space.

B_Extractor.name: Extractor
B_Extractor.desc: This building can only be assembled over a resource deposit, which it will then drill into to extract raw ore automatically.

B_LiquidExtractor.name: Liquid Extractor
B_LiquidExtractor.desc: This building can only be assembled over a geothermal pool, from which it will extract pure fluid automatically.

B_SolarPanel.name: Solar Plant
B_SolarPanel.desc: This building converts electromagnetic radiation into electric current, supplying the base's power grid. It takes up space and must be oriented correctly, but it operates without fuel.

B_Warehouse.name: Warehouse
B_Warehouse.desc: This building is suitable for storing solid materials, which can be easily moved in and out by drones.

B_Organiser.name: Drone Dispatcher
B_Organiser.desc: This building can monitor multiple drone routes and merge them into full-fledged production chains. The backbone of a well-organized pipeline.

B_Chest.name: Trading Box
B_Chest.desc: This small box let anyone from the same corporation than the base owner leave, or take, gifts.

B_Cistern.name: Cistern
B_Cistern.desc: This building is suitable for storing liquid or semi-fluid materials.

B_FTLCistern.name: Mag-Plasma Cistern
B_FTLCistern.desc: This building is suitable for storing active mag-plasma.

B_FTLGatherer.name: Cosmic Condenser
B_FTLGatherer.desc: [NOT IMPL] Building that produce mag-plasma slowly, based on some local properties (maybe proximity from other condensator, and space station reduce production)

B_Crystaliser.name: Crystallizer
B_Crystaliser.desc: This building uses chemical solutions to generate crystals at exceptionally high speeds compared to their natural formation process.

B_Farm.name: Xenic Farm
B_Farm.desc: This building provides an appropriate environment to cultivate living organisms.

B_AntimatterSynthPH.name: Antimatter Synthesizer
B_AntimatterSynthPH.desc: This building consumes huge amounts of electricity to produce trace amounts of antimatter, which is still rather remarkable.

B_LaboPH.name: Laboratory
B_LaboPH.desc: This building contains a compact Ninmah laboratory with everything needed to analyze exotic substances.

B_Connector.name: Cable
B_Connector.desc: This simple cable composes the electricty grid that power every settlement in the Atlas galaxy.

B_ElectricPillar.name: Pylon
B_ElectricPillar.desc: This pole can support electric cables and connect the base's power grid to another building. To plug a cable into it, target the top of the pylon and press E.

B_Generator.name: Fuel Power Plant
B_Generator.desc: This building consumes carbon-based fuel to produce electric current, supplying the base's power grid. It is effective and compact, but resource-intensive.

B_HeatRecouper.name: Heat Capacitor
B_HeatRecouper.desc: [NOT IMPL] reduce temperature around the base. Produce power based on current temperature

B_PowerTransmitter.name: Power Transmitter
B_PowerTransmitter.desc: This building exploits cutting-edge shield technology and gravitational spacetime deformation to beam electricity without loss between different bases in the same stellar system.

B_Battery.name: Battery Array
B_Battery.desc: This building stores chemical energy when the power grid produces excess power. It can then readily convert that energy back to electricity during spikes in consumption.

B_Factory1.name: Assembler
B_Factory1.desc: This building assembles basic resources into industrial compounds. As an automated alternative to the manual workshop, it can function continuously with no human input, which makes it appropriate for large-scale production.

B_Factory2.name: Factory
B_Factory2.desc: The biggest ship materials can only be built in a custom large-scale factory that consumes a lot of energy.

B_Smelter.name: Smelter
B_Smelter.desc: This building converts raw ore into industrial ingots. As an automated alternative to the manual smelter, it can function continuously with no human input, which makes it appropriate for large-scale production.

B_SmelterSA.name: Micro-Furnace
B_SmelterSA.desc: Meitner & Karls have refined the process of turning ore into ingots with this micro-furnace. Smaller and faster than a regular smelter, it's the perfect accessory for your days of heavy manual crafting.

B_Chemical.name: Chemical Factory
B_Chemical.desc: This building assembles chemical elements into industrial compounds. It can function continuously with no human input, which makes it appropriate for large-scale production.

B_BottlingPlant.name: Bottling Plant
B_BottlingPlant.desc: This building packages liquid or semi-fluid products into standardized containers.

B_RecyclingPlant.name: Recycling Plant
B_RecyclingPlant.desc: This building processes scraps to extract their various components and prepare them for industrial reuse.

B_ShuttlePad.name: Shuttle Landing Pad
B_ShuttlePad.desc: This platform allows a shuttle to dock on the base, transporting goods into and out of it.

B_LandingPad.name: Landing Pad
B_LandingPad.desc: This platform allows a ship to dock on the base and connect to its distribution system to transfer fuel or other fluids.

B_Shipyard.name: Shipyard
B_Shipyard.desc: This platform allows a ship to dock on the base to have its structure modified and its modules replaced. It is similar to the shipyards of space stations.

B_PathwayHolder.name: Pathway Stand
B_PathwayHolder.desc:
This towering structure can keep an existing spacetime tunnel stable, even in the gravitational well of a planet. A ship can travel through such a pathway with their regular FTL engine.
Requires the deployment of a [PathwayAmmo] with a [PathwayPuncher] at the exit point.

B_AtmoDome.name: Atmo-dome Generator
B_AtmoDome.desc: [NOT IMPL] Building that reduces or negates entirely weather pattern and environmental stuff around the base and in a tube above it, up to space, allowing ship that are not equipped for it to interact with the base.

B_Observatory.name: Observatory
B_Observatory.desc: [NOT IMPL] Building that allow either the owner or its corporation to see invisible FTL pathways and remove the required level to use an FTL pathway, up to a certain range.

B_Hypergate.name: Hypergate
B_Hypergate.desc: [NOT IMPL] Building that allows the creation of a tunnel toward a hypergate beacon from the same corporation. Consume a lot of power.

B_SunStation.name: Sun Station
B_SunStation.desc: [NOT IMPL] Space Building that produce a huge amount of power from a nearby star. Damage itself when its batteries are full.

B_DecoTest.name: Test deco
B_DecoTest.desc: This building is only a decoration, but style is often the difference between good contractors and great ones.

B_DecoPirate.name: Pirate Hologram
B_DecoPirate.desc: This building is only a decoration, but style is often the difference between good contractors and great ones.

B_DecoTranscendance.name: Transcendance Hologram
B_DecoTranscendance.desc: This building is only a decoration, but style is often the difference between good contractors and great ones.

B_DecoPlanet.name: Planet Hologram
B_DecoPlanet.desc: This building is only a decoration, but style is often the difference between good contractors and great ones.

B_DecoSakura.name: Sakura Hologram
B_DecoSakura.desc: This building is only a decoration, but style is often the difference between good contractors and great ones.

B_DecoSnake.name: Snake Hologram
B_DecoSnake.desc: This building is only a decoration, but style is often the difference between good contractors and great ones.

B_DecoDiver.name: Diver Hologram
B_DecoDiver.desc: This building is only a decoration, but style is often the difference between good contractors and great ones.

EmptyInventory.name: Empty
EmptyInventory.desc: There is nothing in your ship's hold.

Deprecated.name: Debris
Deprecated.desc: The remnant of an old thing. There is no way to restore it, now, nor to find out what it was. The universe has changed and it is no longer needed.

Credits.name: Credits
Credits.desc: Credits are the shared currency of the Atlas Galaxy, issued by the Interstellar Union Of Banks under The Republic's supervision.

CorpoCredits.name: Corpo Credits
CorpoCredits.desc: These credits are owned by a corporation rather than an individual.

SoftMoney.name: NXT
SoftMoney.desc:
NXT is the currency to unlock additional visuals for your character, your ship or you space base.

The visuals unlocked are guaranteed to be completely free of any advantage (unless you consider looking good being an advantage).

NXT are gained by playing and progressing in SpaceCraft.

HardMoney.name: PXE
HardMoney.desc:
PXE can be bought for real money in order to unlock some visuals.

It is similar to NXT except that visuals unlock this way will be kept forever even if your reset your account.

Buying PXE is not mandatory but helps supporting Shiro Games, the game studio that made SpaceCraft.

CorpoReputation.name: Reputation
CorpoReputation.desc: As each Corpo completes its various requests, their reputation across the galaxy improves.

LicensePoints.name: License Points
LicensePoints.desc: Regulating access to industrial licenses, these points are issued by The Republic's Universal Licensing Agency.

PatchDescHolder.desc: This enhancement can be permanently attached to a spaceship module. If the module is recycled, its attached patch has a 50% chance of being preserved.

Blueprint.name: Blueprint
Blueprint.desc: This detailed plan contains all the necessary instructions to build a specific ship module. It can be read by a human or by a machine.

MaxSpeed.name: Max Speed
MaxSpeed.desc: Max Speed is the speed of the ship at full throttle, with no boosters, in a vacuum. Different travel media will affect the ship’s actual speed.

MaxBoostSpeed.name: Max Boost Speed
MaxBoostSpeed.desc: Max Boost Speed is the speed of the ship at full throttle, while using the boosters, in a vacuum. Different travel media will affect the ship’s actual speed.

PowerStorage.name: Power Storage
PowerStorage.desc: Power Storage is the amount of electrical charge that can be stored in the ship's battery.

SolidStorage.name: Solid Storage
SolidStorage.desc: Storing solid materials is mostly a question of efficient organization.

OutputStorage.name: Output Storage
OutputStorage.desc: In a factory, the Output Storage limits the maximum amount of the current craft’s outputs that can be stored in the factory.

InputStorage.name: Input Storage
InputStorage.desc: In a factory, the Input Storage limits the maximum amount of the current craft’s inputs that can be stored in the factory.

PersonaEquipCost.name: Equip Cost
PersonaEquipCost.desc: The Equip Cost of contractor equipment limits how much a single contractor can hold at once.

ItemDurability.name: Durability
ItemDurability.desc: Nothing is eternal. This item slowly degrades every time it is used, until it eventually no longer functions.

Temperature.name: Temperature
Temperature.desc: The ship’s current average Temperature is a function of how much heat the ship stores compared to its Heat Capacity. If the Temperature gets too high or too low, the ship will be damaged.

HeatProtection.name: Heat Protection
HeatProtection.desc: Heat Protection improves the suit’s ability to shield a contractor from high temperatures.

ColdProtection.name: Cold Protection
ColdProtection.desc: Cold Protection improves the suit’s ability to shield a contractor from freezing temperatures.

RadioactivityProtection.name: Radioactivity Protection
RadioactivityProtection.desc: Radioactivity Protection shields the contractor from all harmful radiation, up to a certain range.

BeaconRange.name: Range
BeaconRange.desc: The amount of resources in range of the beacon will be incorporated into its quantum signature.

MaxTechXP.name: Experience Capacity
MaxTechXP.desc:
Experience in the Technology branch is acquired by building new bases and crafting components in automated factories.

Each base has a maximum amount of Technology Experience that can be acquired in this way.

PatchSlots.name: Patch Slots
PatchSlots.desc: This item can receive patches to improve its performance. Equipped patches cannot be removed without destroying the whole item.

ShuttleRange.name: Shuttle Range
ShuttleRange.desc: How far the shuttle can travel.

Frame.name: Frame
Frame.desc: Frame corresponds to a ship’s structural capacity to not break apart under its own Weight. It increases the ship’s Integrity.

ShipWeight.name: Weight
ShipWeight.desc: Weight is a measure of the ship’s total mass. It impacts its structural Integrity, as well as its ability to move.

Hull.name: Hull
Hull.desc: Hull represents your ship’s overall resistance. When a ship is out of Hull points, it is out of order and requires towing.

HeatCapacity.name: Heat Capacity
HeatCapacity.desc: Heat Capacity corresponds to the amount of energy needed to raise the ship’s temperature. The bigger the ship, the higher its Heat Capacity, and the more heat it can absorb before overheating.

HeatInterfaceMaterial.name: Material Heat Conductivity
HeatInterfaceMaterial.desc: A material’s Heat Conductivity determines how fast heat can move through it. A hull made of highly Heat Conductive materials will have a greater Heat Interface, and will change Temperature faster depending on the environment.

HeatInterfaceShip.name: Heat Interface
HeatInterfaceShip.desc: Heat Interface corresponds to the amount of heat transfer between the ship and its environment. It changes with the size of the ship, but also with the materials used in its construction. The greater the Heat Interface, the faster the ship will be affected by environmental temperatures.

SystemSupport.name: System Support
SystemSupport.desc: System Support is the number of secondary systems available in your ship to service its modules. If System Requirements become greater than System Support, the ship will have a harder time evacuating heat properly from its module, leading to faster overheating.

SystemRequirement.name: System Requirements
SystemRequirement.desc: System Requirements represent how much help a module needs from secondary systems to function properly without overheating.

SystemEfficiency.name: System Efficiency
SystemEfficiency.desc:
System Efficiency represents the extent to which the ship's system support is sufficient to meet its requirements.

System Efficiency will affect various systems such as Heat or Engine.

EngineForce.name: Force
EngineForce.desc: An engine’s Force determines how much weight can be moved by the engine. If the total force of all of a ship’s engines is less than its total weight, the ship will not be able to move.

EngineThrust.name: Thrust
EngineThrust.desc: The Thrust of an engine determines how fast it can move and accelerate. Multiple engines produce less thrust than the sum of their individual thrusts, and heavy ships move slower than lighter ones.

EngineThrustBonusUpwards.name: Liftoff Speed Bonus
EngineThrustBonusUpwards.desc: An engine optimized for going up will make the ship move faster when attempting to leave orbit.

FTOilStorage.name: Mag-Plasma Storage
FTOilStorage.desc: This item can hold mag-plasma, the physics-defying material allowing for safe supraluminal travel.

FTLEngineJumpCost.name: FTL Jump Cost
FTLEngineJumpCost.desc: The jump cost of an FTL engine determines the base cost of a FTL jump, without taking the ship’s Weight into account.

FTLEngineWeightCapacity.name: FTL Weight Capacity
FTLEngineWeightCapacity.desc: An FTL engine’s weight capacity determines how much the ship’s weight impacts the cost of a FTL jump. The greater the weight capacity, the less the weight of the ship will matter.

FakeFTLEngineClass.name: FTL Engine Class
FakeFTLEngineClass.desc: More performant FTL engines do the same route with less mag-plasma, but they require a greater expertise.

FakeFTLOptimalMaxWeight.name: Optimal up to
FakeFTLOptimalMaxWeight.desc: Each FTL engine is calibrated for transporting a certain mass of matter. Using the wrong engine for your ship will cause an overconsumption of mag-plasma, leading to a potential increase of supraluminic damage.

FTOilConsumptionFactor.name: Mag-Plasma Consumption
FTOilConsumptionFactor.desc: This FTL Engine has been tweaked to consume less mag-plasma per jump.

FTOilConsumptionFactorOnVisitedPath.name: Mag-Plasma Cost On Known Paths
FTOilConsumptionFactorOnVisitedPath.desc: This FTL Engine has been tweaked to consume less mag-plasma when jumping through a known path.

Integrity.name: Integrity
Integrity.desc: Integrity represents the extent to which the ship’s Frame is sufficient for its Weight. A low Integrity rating weakens the ship’s Hull.

SpotLightRange.name: Light Range
SpotLightRange.desc: This module's light reaches up to the specified range in the darkness ahead.

SpotLightPower.name: Luminous Power
SpotLightPower.desc: This bright module activates in darkness to light up the environment in front of the ship.

EnableNavigator.name: Unlock path planning
EnableNavigator.desc: When this module is on the ship, right click on a star in the galaxy map to plan a path.

SteeringStrength.name: Steering Strength
SteeringStrength.desc: The Steering Strength of a part is used to compute the Maneuvrability of the ship. The higher its steering strength, the more a part contributes to steering the ship.

Maneuvrability.name: Maneuverability
Maneuvrability.desc: Maneuverability is a measure of how efficient the steering strength of the wings and thrusters are to move the weight of the ship.

AccelerationTime.name: Acceleration Time
AccelerationTime.desc: Acceleration time is the time the ship takes to accelerate from an immobile state to its max speed. Better thrusters and lighter hull can improve this value.

CorpoUpkeep.name: Upkeep
CorpoUpkeep.desc: A corpo’s Upkeep is the amount of credits the corpo must pay every day to sustain its operations. Each permit you unlock will generate more Upkeep. You can return permits to reduce upkeep.

CorpoCanRemoveUser.name: Can fire members
CorpoCanRemoveUser.desc: Can remove members from the same group or any group with a lower rank.

CorpoCanPromoteUser.name: Can promote members
CorpoCanPromoteUser.desc: Can promote members from the same group or any group with a lower rank.

CorpoCanDemoteUser.name: Can demote members
CorpoCanDemoteUser.desc: Can demote members from the same group or any group with a lower rank.

CorpoCanEditInternalInfos.name: Can edit internal info
CorpoCanEditInternalInfos.desc: Can modify the text appearing on the corpo menu of all members.

CorpoCanTransferAssets.name: Can transfer assets
CorpoCanTransferAssets.desc: Can transfer ships and bases owned by the corpo to corpo members

CorpoCanManageBases.name: Can manage bases
CorpoCanManageBases.desc: Can deploy bases for the corpo and abandon bases owned by the corpo

CorpoCanManagePermits.name: Can unlock and cancel corpo permits

CorpoCanManageStorage.name: Can buy and upgrade station storage

CorpoDefaultGroup.name: Default Group
CorpoDefaultGroup.desc: This is the default group for new members.

MiningPower.name: Mining Power
MiningPower.desc: Mining Power is an estimation of the speed at which your laser will pierce a piece of mineral. Some minerals are harder than others, but the higher the power, the faster the mining.

MiningTier.name: Mining Tier
MiningTier.desc: Every mineral node has a Tier. Only a laser with a strong enough Mining Tier relative to a given node will be able to mine it.

MiningLastYield.name: Last Yield
MiningLastYield.desc: When it makes the last hit on a resource node, this module produces one additional yield.

MiningOutputIncrease.name: Increased Output
MiningOutputIncrease.desc: This module mines more of the main resources present in a node. This increased output does not apply to rare finds.

MiningSecondaryChanceIncrease.name: Rare Find Probability
MiningSecondaryChanceIncrease.desc: This module increases the chance to find rare minerals when mining.

MaxScanningTier.name: Scanning Tier
MaxScanningTier.desc: Every mineral node has a Tier. Only a scanner with a strong enough Scanning Tier relative to a given node will be able to scan it.

FluidStorage.name: Reservoir Size
FluidStorage.desc: The Reservoir Size is a measure of how much fluid can be held inside this item.

FluidPumpingSpeed.name: Flow Rate
FluidPumpingSpeed.desc: Flow Rate is the amount of fluid gathered every second that the skill is used in the appropriate environment.

ActiveHeatGeneration.name: Heat Generation
ActiveHeatGeneration.desc: This skill will heat up the ship when used.

BoosterHeatGeneration.name: Boost Heat Generation
BoosterHeatGeneration.desc: Active boosters heat up the ship.

FakeAllActiveHeatGeneration.name: Heat Generation
FakeAllActiveHeatGeneration.desc: This modifier affects every form of active heat generation of the attached module.

HeatGeneration.name: Heat Generation
HeatGeneration.desc: Heat Generation modules automatically activate to heat up the ship when it is getting too cold.

HeatDissipation.name: Heat Dissipation
HeatDissipation.desc: Heat Dissipation modules automatically activate to cool down the ship when it is getting too hot.

EngineHeatDissipation.name: Engine Heat Dissipation
EngineHeatDissipation.desc: A specialized engine can use the natural heat conduction of the atmosphere to cool down faster while in motion.

BatteryChargeSpeed.name: Max Charge Speed
BatteryChargeSpeed.desc: No matter how much current you send them, batteries can never charge faster than their Max Charge Speed.

BatteryEfficiency.name: Theoretical Efficiency
BatteryEfficiency.desc: Batteries are not perfectly efficient: a part of the electrical current used to charge them is lost. A battery’s actual efficiency depends on its Theoretical Efficiency, but also decreases with the amount of current it receives.

BatteryWastage.name: Self-Discharge
BatteryWastage.desc: A battery that is left unused discharges on its own over time.

BoostThrust.name: Boost Thrust
BoostThrust.desc: This module adds thrust to the engine when boosters are active, which increases Max Boost Speed.

PowerProduction.name: Power Generation
PowerProduction.desc: Power Generation refers to production of electrical currents that can be used to power the ship.

PowerUsage.name: Power Usage
PowerUsage.desc: This module consumes electrical power when it is in use.

FakeAllPowerConsumption.name: Power Consumption
FakeAllPowerConsumption.desc: This modifier affects every form of power consumption of the attached module.

EngineConsumption.name: Power Consumption
EngineConsumption.desc: Engine Consumption is the amount of electrical current required to use the engines at full throttle.

EngineConsumptionReductionInDark.name: Night Power Consumption
EngineConsumptionReductionInDark.desc: Through clever electromagnetic trickery, an ionic thruster can sometimes be optimized to consume less energy under cover of darkness.

FTLMaxCritProbaReduction.name: FTL Crit Chance
FTLMaxCritProbaReduction.desc: This module reduces the probability of critical damages during a supraluminal jump.

FTLDamageAbsorption.name: FTL Damage Reduction
FTLDamageAbsorption.desc: This module reduces the amount of damage taken during FTL travel.

HullImpactDamageReduction.name: Hull Impact Damage
HullImpactDamageReduction.desc: This module reduces damage to the hull inflicted by collisions with the ground, asteroids, and other space objects.

RadarRange.name: Max Range
RadarRange.desc: The radar’s Max Range is the range at which it detects minerals and other resources on the surface of a planet. Major landmarks and unique locations can be detected from farther away.

WreckFinder.name: Wreck Finder
WreckFinder.desc: Radar capabilities are enhanced to detect ship wrecks from a much longer distance away.

ShieldMaxCharge.name: Shield Max Charge
ShieldMaxCharge.desc: The Max Charge is the maximum amount of damage that can be absorbed by a shield before it turns off, not counting any shield regeneration.

DamageNegation.name: Damage Negation
DamageNegation.desc: Damage Negation is the amount of damage that is ignored by a shield upon each separate time it is hit.

RegenPowerConsumption.name: Regen Power Consumption
RegenPowerConsumption.desc: A shield’s power consumption increases while regenerating. This is its Regen Power Consumption.

RegenDelay.name: Regen Delay
RegenDelay.desc: Before it can regenerate, a shield must not take any damage for a certain amount of time. This is its Regen Delay.

RegenSpeed.name: Regen Speed
RegenSpeed.desc: A shield’s Regen Speed is the speed at which it regains its charge during regeneration.

Cooldown.name: Cooldown
Cooldown.desc: The Cooldown is the minimum time between skill activation.

AmmoConsumption.name: Ammo Consumption
AmmoConsumption.desc: Ammo Consumption is the amount of ammo used by each skill activation.

CurrentAgility_Display.name: Agility
CurrentAgility_Display.desc: Agility is the ship’s ability to steer and avoid dangerous projectiles coming towards it. It affects the ship’s dodge chance in combat.

AllowDismantleShipWrecks.name: Wreck Dismantling
AllowDismantleShipWrecks.desc: This item can dismantle heavily damaged structures and ships.

MaxConnectorAmount.name: Max Connection
MaxConnectorAmount.desc: Max Connection is the maximum number of cables that can be plugged into this building for Energy Grid management purposes.

EnergyDemand.name: Energy Demand
EnergyDemand.desc: This building requires Energy to function properly. It must be connected to the Energy grid of its base, and will slow down if its Energy needs are not met.

EnergyOffer.name: Energy Generation
EnergyOffer.desc: This building helps to generate Energy for its base. It must be connected to the base’s Energy grid to give this benefit.

EnergyStockCapacity.name: Energy Capacity
EnergyStockCapacity.desc: The Energy Capacity of a battery building is the maximum amount of Energy it can store when the Energy grid is over-producing.

EnergyTransferCapacity.name: Transfer Capacity
EnergyTransferCapacity.desc: The Transfer Capacity is the maximum amount of Energy that can transit through a transmitter building.

FuelConversion.name: Fuel Power
FuelConversion.desc: Fuel Power is the amount of Fuel in a single unit of the chosen item. The higher the Fuel Power is, the slower the item will be consumed.

FuelConsumption.name: Fuel Consumption
FuelConsumption.desc: Fuel Consumption is the amount of Fuel Power consumed every second.

BuildPointsCost.name: Footprint
BuildPointsCost.desc: Most buildings use Footprint. The total Footprint used on a base must always be lower than its Footprint Capacity.

DecoPointsCost.name: Deco Points
DecoPointsCost.desc: Decoration buildings use Deco Points instead of Footprint. The total Deco Point used on a base must always be lower than its Decoration Capacity.

MaxBuildPoints.name: Footprint Capacity
MaxBuildPoints.desc: The Footprint Capacity is a measure of the amount of buildings that a base is allowed to hold.

MaxDecoPoints.name: Decoration Capacity
MaxDecoPoints.desc: The Decoration Capacity is a measure of the amount of decoration building that a base is allowed to hold.

DroneRouteCount.name: Drone Route Count
DroneRouteCount.desc: This building can hold more than one drone route.

FakeGiveTechXP.name: Generate Technology XP
FakeGiveTechXP.desc: When this building crafts an item, it also contributes to technology experience generation on the base.

FarmPlantGrowthSpeed.name: Productive Metabolic Speed
FarmPlantGrowthSpeed.desc:
Productive metabolic speed affects the duration of a plant growth without changing the hourly amount of products generated.

A small productive metabolic speed leads to longer growth time but higher product yield in the end.

FarmPlantGrowthSpeedMult.name: Productive Metabolic Speed
FarmPlantGrowthSpeedMult.desc:
Productive metabolic speed affects the duration of a plant growth without changing the hourly amount of products generated.

A small productive metabolic speed leads to longer growth time but higher product yield in the end.

FarmPlantFruitProductionSpeed.name: Germ Quantity
FarmPlantFruitProductionSpeed.desc: Germ quantity affects how many germ products will be gathered at the end of a plant growth.

FarmPlantFruitProductionSpeedMult.name: Germ Quantity
FarmPlantFruitProductionSpeedMult.desc: Germ quantity affects how many germ products will be gathered at the end of a plant growth.

FarmPlantExtraProductionSpeed.name: Byproduct Quantity
FarmPlantExtraProductionSpeed.desc: Byproduct quantity affects how many non-germ products will be gathered at the end of a plant growth.

FarmPlantExtraProductionSpeedMult.name: Byproduct Quantity
FarmPlantExtraProductionSpeedMult.desc: Byproduct quantity affects how many non-germ products will be gathered at the end of a plant growth.

FarmPlantAllSpeed.name: Growth and Production Speed
FarmPlantAllSpeed.desc: Growth speed affects how long a plant will take to reach its gatherable state.

FarmPlantDeathSpeed.name: Decay Speed
FarmPlantDeathSpeed.desc: Decay speed affects how long a plant will take to die if its requirements are not met.

FarmPlantDissolveDead.name: Dissolve Dead Plants
FarmPlantDissolveDead.desc: The affected plant will dissolve on its own if it is already dead.

FarmPlantSeedGerminateSpeed.name: Seed Growth Speed
FarmPlantSeedGerminateSpeed.desc: Germination speed affects how long a plant will take to complete its germination phase.

FarmPlantSeedDeathSpeed.name: Seed Death Speed
FarmPlantSeedDeathSpeed.desc: Decay speed affects how long a plant will take to die if its requirements are not met. This effect only apply during the germination phase.

FarmPlantLiquidConsumption.name: Liquid Consumption
FarmPlantLiquidConsumption.desc: Liquid Consumption affects how much of its liquid requirement a plant will consume over time.

FarmPlantLiquidConsumptionMult.name: Liquid Consumption
FarmPlantLiquidConsumptionMult.desc: Liquid Consumption affects how much of its liquid requirement a plant will consume over time.

FarmPlantSupplementConsumption.name: Supplement Consumption
FarmPlantSupplementConsumption.desc: Supplement Consumption affects how much of the various fertilizers and other supplements used in its area a plant will consume over time.

FarmPlantSupplementConsumptionMult.name: Supplement Consumption
FarmPlantSupplementConsumptionMult.desc: Supplement Consumption affects how much of the various fertilizers and other supplements used in its area a plant will consume over time.

FarmPlantIgnoreMinRequirement.name: Ignore One Requirement
FarmPlantIgnoreMinRequirement.desc: The affected plant will be able to grow even if a single one of its requirements is not met.

FarmPlantSetLightToUV.name: Set Light to UV
FarmPlantSetLightToUV.desc: The affected plant will react as if it was under UV Lights conditions.

PlayerStructureAccess.name: Structure access
PlayerStructureAccess.desc: Gain access to the assembly tab to move, add, and remove parts of your ship in the hangar.

PlayerShipDecoAccess.name: Decoration access
PlayerShipDecoAccess.desc: Gain access to paints to decorate your ship in the hangar

PlayerShipDecalAccess.name: Advanced Decoration access
PlayerShipDecalAccess.desc: Gain access to decals and stencils to decorate your ship in the hangar

SoldCapBonus.name: Increase Company Reputation
SoldCapBonus.desc: Increase company reputation and the amount of resources you can sell to them.

Exploration.name: Exploration
Exploration.desc:
Exploration is the art of traveling to unknown places, going farther than anyone else before, and pushing the frontiers of human settlement in the galaxy.
Complete *planet resource discovery* to increase your Exploration XP.

Technology.name: Technology
Technology.desc:
Technology is all about improving industrial output to build more, better, and faster.
Earn *Technology XP* in your space base’s command center by producing components through automation. You also get extra *XP* when deploying a new base building for the first time.

Science.name: Science
Science.desc:
Understanding the mysteries of the universe won’t always lead to immediate benefits, but it's the only way forward for humanity, and for the Company Science program.
Study minerals and organic resources at the *Laboratory* to increase your science XP.

Social.name: Social
Social.desc:
The Galaxy is a global competition between Corpos. Your Social level represents your personal achievements accumulated over time.
Earn social XP by joining a Corpo, then complete *Corpo Requests* to increase your social XP.

Combat.name: Combat
Combat.desc:
Combat will allow you to fight in PvE and coop against Space Pirate foes. It will be real-time, but will not require intense action.
*Combat is not part of the initial Early Access release*, but we are working on it and will keep you posted!
